_inspectorSnowReport.Report:
3/11/26 8:00 AM Update:

Good morning, snow lovers and professional powder inspectors of Crystal Mountain! Overnight we picked up a modest 2 inches of fresh snow. Yes, we were promised a bit more, but hey, that’s still a fresh frosting on top of the 8–10 inches we've stacked up over the last couple days. Which means conditions remain somewhere between “pretty darn good” and “cancel your afternoon meetings.” The real headline though? The next storm has entered the chat. Snow is expected to ramp up through the day as a major system rolls in and the forecast is calling for a lot more snow. Like… the kind where you start mentally preparing your legs and wondering if your snorkel is waxed.

In fact, a blizzard warning has been issued, which meteorologists translate loosely as: “Things are about to get rowdy.”

So enjoy the warm-up laps this morning. Visibility is still cooperating, the snow is soft and it’s the calm before what could be a serious powder party. Buckle up, the big one is on the way. 

Now go stretch your quads, charge your GoPro and locate the friend you ride with on storm days. You're going to need them.

Join us today and we will see you out there!

Events: 
  • The Campbell Basin Rail Jam has moved to next Saturday March 14th!  From 11am-2pm throw down your cleanest lines, stomp your best tricks, and compete for some seriously sweet prizes from our stacked sponsors. Not competing? No stress — pull up, hype your crew, and catch all the action from Campbell Basin Lodge (elite viewing vibes included).
Notes for today:
  1. Summit House will be closed March 11 and Rafters will be closed March 11 and 12 for private events. 
  2. $199 Spring 3-Day Passes are on sale now! Ski for just $67 a day, any day of the week, for the rest of the season!
  3. $62 Day tickets continue every Tuesday for the remainder of the season!
  4. Did you know it just takes 5 Days to be entered to win a 26/27 Ikon Pass? Track your days on the Ikon App and register for RIDE TO WIN
  5. Looking to save time and make the most of the day? Consider grabbing a Daily Reserve Upgrade! Available on weekends and holidays, this upgrade grants access to priority lift lines, getting you on the lift faster and allowing you to complete more laps. 
  6. Paid reservations can only be cancelled by emailing SP+ at  CrystalMtnParking@spplus.com with Reservation #, First Name, and Last Name. While you may not receive an immediate reply, the cancellation will be processed. The Crystal Mountain Contact Center cannot process these cancellations.
  7. In case of on-mountain emergency: Call 360-663-3064 or 911

Mountain Weather 3/10/2026 1:00pm 

Forecaster: Bradford Swanson
 
Synopsis: A furious winter storm is knocking at our door with a serious vengeance. The fireworks kick off Tuesday night and will continue to build, eventually bringing us to climax on Friday. This is a very strong system that has the potential to deliver many feet of snow to our mountain. The storm track is predominantly out of the Southwest, meaning Rainier will take some of our snow, but there should be more than enough to get around the block and deliver a proper load to Crystal. As expected with such a powerful storm, the wind will also be a significant factor this week.  Wednesday afternoon, winds will start to get cranky, then overnight they will get downright angry, with the potential to top 70mph.


Tuesday Night:
Sky Cover: Cloudy and snowing
Freezing Elevation: 2,500’
12hr Precip: 3-6”
Wind Trends: Light Southwest
Wednesday:
Sky Cover: Cloudy and snowing
Freezing Elevation: 5,000’
12hr Precip: 7-11”
Wind Trends: Moderate to Strong West Southwest, Gusting 50mph in the late afternoon
Wednesday Night:
Sky Cover: Cloudy and snowing
Freezing Elevation: 6,300’
12hr Precip: 6-9” 
Wind Trends: Extreme West, Gusting 70+mph
Extended:   
Thursday and Friday, the wind should decrease slightly but will remain a factor throughout the forecast period. Hold on tight, everyone. We are smack in the bullseye of a very serious and volatile winter storm. The combination of strong winds, high water equivalent, and upside-down temperatures will make this a very tumultuous system.

*Daily Summaries: 12hr period representing 4am-4pm or 4pm-4am data
*Freezing Elevation (ft): Approximate elevation above sea level where the air temperature reaches 32 degree F

Uphill Travel Update: See the above banner for the current status. Please visit guest services to get your 25/26 uphill travel card. This must be renewed free of charge for the season. Uphill travel may be prohibited at any time as mountain operations teams conduct avalanche mitigation. Refer to the "No Uphill Travel" lights around the base area and the Mountain Report page indicating the status of the designated route. These lights are located by the Gondola trail map, at the bottom of Chinook, at the bottom of Discovery, and at the top of the Quicksilver lift shack. For more information about uphill travel, please visit the uphill travel page. 

25/26 WINTER PROGRAMS, CLINICS, AND NECESSITIES

  • Ski and Ride School: Explore our programs and find the right fit for you.
  • AIARE Course & Certification: Ski the backcountry with expert knowledge and certification
